examples: The most distinctive attribute of this tradition is the mechanistic, materialistic conception of humanity.
During the 1980s the U.S. became a very materialistic society.
Many said that unemployment, while extremely painful, had improved them in some ways: they had become less materialistic and more financially prudent; they were more aware of the struggles of others.
"I'm really not materialistic, but I just didn't think it would work out, " she said.
Much of the animus against the free enterprise system is that it encourages shallow, materialistic values.
